aeroecto-by-flight:
**What it does:** Fully automates the Ectofuntus for Prayer training: banks bones, grinds them into bonemeal at the grinder, collects buckets of slime from the pool below, and offers bonemeal + slime at the Ectofuntus altar, cycling through the whole process. Supports all bone types.

**Requirements:** Access to Port Phasmatys and the Ectofuntus (Ghosts Ahoy started, Ghostspeak amulet worn), buckets and bones in the bank, and Ecto-tokens/energy barrier fee. Members. Agility 58+ optionally unlocks a wall shortcut. Castle Wars banking option needs a Ring of dueling.

**Setup:** Have your chosen bones and buckets banked; wear a Ghostspeak amulet and keep an Ectophial for teleporting. Choose bone type and bank location (Port Phasmatys or Castle Wars) in the GUI. Downloads the Ectofuntus map and GUI background on first run.

**Features:**
- Bone-type selector covering 20 bone types and a bank-location selector (Port Phasmatys / Castle Wars)
- Full location state machine handling the multi-level Ectofuntus basement navigation
- Ectophial teleport handling and Ring of dueling charge tracking for Castle Wars
- Optional Agility wall shortcut when level 58+
- Configurable max runtime, breaks, and sleeps with real-runtime tracking
- Progress report (runtime, bones used, XP gained, per-hour rates)
aerofisher-by-flight:
**What it does:** A multi-location fishing bot that fishes a chosen spot type, then either banks the catch, powerfishes (drops it), cooks it, or processes it, handling travel between fishing spot and bank/range automatically. Covers 16 locations from Draynor shrimp to Anglerfish, Karambwan, and Infernal/Sacred eels.

**Requirements:** Fishing level and equipment appropriate to the chosen fish (net/rod/lobster cage/harpoon, bait/feathers, coins for boats). Membership for most locations (F2P world list included for free spots). Karamja/Zanaris routes use fairy rings; some spots need quest access.

**Setup:** Bring the correct fishing tool(s) and any bait for your target; carry coins (2500+) if a location requires the Karamja boat. Pick location, fish, and mode in the GUI. Downloads a GUI background image on first run.

**Features:**
- Location selector (16 spots) with dynamically filtered fish and mode (Bank/Powerfish/Process) options
- Cook-fish toggle, barehanded-fishing toggle, and Dragon harpoon special-attack toggle (context-sensitive per location)
- Automatic world hopping when nearby player chat is detected (member and F2P world lists)
- Boat boarding (Port Sarim/Musa Point) and fairy-ring travel (Karamja/Zanaris)
- Runs-to-bank on combat/low-HP detection
- Custom hybrid experimental walking and configurable breaks/sleeps
- Progress report with fish caught, XP, and per-hour rates
aerokebbits-by-flight:
**What it does:** Automates falconry hunting in the Piscatoris Falconry Area: throws the gyr falcon at kebbits, retrieves the falcon and catch, then drops furs/bones to keep hunting, looping until shutdown. Targets Spotted, Dark, and/or Dashing kebbits.

**Requirements:** Hunter level for falconry (43+ for spotted, higher for dark/dashing) and a hired gyr falcon from Matthias. Members. Must be inside the Piscatoris Falconry Area.

**Setup:** Hire a falcon from Matthias and stand in the falconry hunting area. Configuration is edited directly in the code's const block (not a GUI): set player name/password, which kebbits to hunt, and time/break settings.

**Features:**
- Target selector via TOHUNT constant (Spotted / Dark / Dashing kebbit)
- Yellow-arrow catch detection to click and retrieve caught kebbits and returning falcon
- Auto-drops furs, raw dashing kebbit, and bones using randomized humanized drop patterns; destroys Kebbity tufts
- World hopping on nearby player chat or when the spot is occupied (configurable)
- Auto-terminates if the falcon is lost
- Hardcoded break/sleep scheduling and world list; progress report with catch count, XP, and success rate
aeromlm-by-flight:
**What it does:** Automates Motherlode Mine: mines pay-dirt veins, deposits it into the hopper, clears rockfall blockages, then collects the mined ores (coal, gold, mithril, adamantite, runite) and nuggets from the sack and banks them at the deposit box. It tracks XP and per-ore counts and world-hops when nearby player chat is detected.

**Requirements:** Members, Motherlode Mine access, a pickaxe (bronze through crystal/infernal, worn or in inventory); upper-level mining additionally requires the higher Mining level for that area; a hammer is picked up in-game to repair struts/clear rockfalls.

**Setup:** Start at the Motherlode Mine with a pickaxe equipped or in the inventory; empty inventory otherwise. Optionally have a dragon pickaxe for the special-attack Mining boost and a Fire cape is auto-swapped to a pickaxe if detected.

**Features:**
- Upper-level mining toggle (higher-level veins)
- Upper-level hopper option
- Dragon pickaxe special-attack Mining boost
- Drop hammer after strut repair to save space
- Automatic world-hopping when player chat is detected
- Configurable max run time, breaks and sleep scheduling
- Live progress report (XP/hr, ores/hr, nuggets)
aeroshards-by-flight:
**What it does:** Trains Prayer in the Ralos/Tal Teklan (Varlamore) area by blessing bones at the Exposed altar, chiselling them into blessed bone shards, and sacrificing the shards at the Libation bowl. It buys jugs of wine from the Gorge bartender to make blessed wine, restores Prayer at the Shrine of Ralos, and travels between Mountain and Gorge via Renu.

**Requirements:** Members, access to the Ralos shrine area (Tal Teklan / the Gorge), a chisel, a stock of blessed bone shards (or bones/sun-kissed bones to process), and coins (script stops below ~5000) for un-noting bones via Virilis.

**Setup:** Carry a chisel and coins; keep bones, blessed bones, noted bones or blessed bone shards available. For sun-kissed bones, store them in the Gorge bank. Zoom is auto-set on start.

**Features:**
- Full Prayer loop: bless, chisel to shards, sacrifice at bowl
- Optional processing of sun-kissed bones from the bank
- Auto-buys jugs of wine from the bartender
- Un-notes bones through Virilis when needed
- Restores Prayer at the Shrine of Ralos
- Configurable max run time, breaks and sleep scheduling
aerowyrms-by-flight:
**What it does:** Kills Wyrms in the Karuulm Slayer Dungeon: walks from above ground down the elevator, over the rock wall into the Wyrm area, detects and attacks Wyrms using pixel-shift plus HP-bar/minimap targeting, eats food, loots drops, and banks at the bank chest. It reports kills, XP and looted items.

**Requirements:** Members, Karuulm Slayer Dungeon access, Boots of stone/boots of brimstone (script terminates if it can't cross the hot rock wall), a melee weapon, and food.

**Setup:** Have combat gear worn, food and (optionally) a dragon battleaxe in the bank; the script withdraws the configured food. Configure food name, amount, and heal-percent in the GUI.

**Features:**
- Configurable food item, quantity, and heal-at HP percent
- Dragon battleaxe special-attack combat boost
- Optional loot-ignoring (skip item pickup)
- Optional arrow retrieval from the ground
- Player-limit world-hopping and hop on player chat
- Automatic banking, eating, and re-walking to the Wyrms
- Configurable max run time, breaks and sleep scheduling
ardy-superheat-iron-by-bigaussie:
**What it does:** Mines iron ore at the Ardougne Monastery mine, casts Superheat Item on each ore to smith iron bars on the spot, then banks the bars and returns to mine again in a continuous loop. Combines Mining, Magic, and Smithing XP.

**Requirements:** Magic level for Superheat Item (43+), Nature runes and fire runes/staff for the spell, a pickaxe, and an Ardougne cloak for teleporting to Kandarin Monastery. Members area (west Ardougne). Ardougne Diary progress determines which cloak tier is used.

**Setup:** Equip an Ardougne cloak (any tier) and have Nature runes plus a pickaxe (iron through dragon supported) in inventory; the script keeps runes and pickaxe when banking. Start near the Monastery iron mine.

**Features:**
- State machine: mine iron, superheat to bars, walk to bank, deposit, teleport back to mine
- Auto-detects Ardougne Diary level to select the correct cloak teleport
- Casts Superheat opportunistically while walking
- Antiban tuned for Total/Magic/Smithing with zoom randomization
- Live progress report (runtime, XP gained, total bars, XP/hour)
- Terminates safely if no cloak is found or on inactivity
barronite-miner-by-bigaussie:
**What it does:** Mines barronite rocks in the Camdozaal (Ruins of Camdozaal) mine, uses the barronite crusher/anvil to break deposits into barronite shards, and banks the results, looping continuously. Tracks Mining XP and shards gathered.

**Requirements:** A hammer and the ability to access Camdozaal (Below Ice Mountain quest). Mining level appropriate for barronite. Membership not explicitly stated in code.

**Setup:** Have a hammer in inventory (kept during banking along with barronite shards). Start in or near the Camdozaal barronite mine; the script uses a built-in web graph for the area.

**Features:**
- State machine: mine, walk to anvil/crusher, crush deposits, walk to bank chest, bank
- Custom experimental hybrid mainscreen/minimap walking
- Tracks and reports rare/ancient relic drops (Barronite head, Imcando hammer, ancient astroscope/carcanet/globe/ledger/treatise)
- Configurable items-to-keep list when depositing
- GUI with account manager, antiban manager, version panel, and WaspLib/API settings
- Live report (runtime, XP, shards, XP/hour and shards/hour estimates)
bigaussie-aio-lunar-spells-by-bigaussie:
**What it does:** Casts Lunar spellbook production spells next to a bank to train Magic and process materials, supporting Bake Pie, Humidify, Spin Flax, Superglass Make, Tan Leather, String Jewellery, Plank Make, and Recharge Dragonstone. It withdraws raw materials plus Astral runes, casts repeatedly, banks the products, and can restock via the Grand Exchange.

**Requirements:** Members, Lunar spellbook (Lunar Diplomacy), the Magic level for the chosen spell, Astral runes (plus Nature/Soul runes and coins for certain spells such as Plank Make), and the relevant raw materials in the bank.

**Setup:** Stand near a bank with the item to process at the top of the inventory; if using a rune pouch it MUST start in the inventory. Choose spell, item, and script speed in the GUI (or the top-of-file defaults).

**Features:**
- Eight selectable Lunar production spells with dozens of item types
- SLOW/NORMAL/FAST/TURBO speed presets
- Optional GE auto-buying of runes and raw materials
- Optional GE selling of finished (noted) items
- Rune pouch / Divine rune pouch and Sawmill voucher support
- Stop-at-level target with logout
- Optional integrated birdhouse runs and Discord webhook alerts
bigaussie-calcified-mining-by-bigaussie:
**What it does:** Mines Calcified rocks in the Cam Torum mine (Varlamore) for blessed bone shards and gems, prioritising the higher-yield wet/waterfall rocks, then processes Calcified deposits into shards at the Cam Torum anvil. It banks gems (via gem bag) and keeps mining tools, reporting Mining XP and resulting Prayer XP.

**Requirements:** Members, Cam Torum mine access (Children of the Sun / Varlamore), a pickaxe, and a hammer (or Imcando hammer) to smash deposits; a gem bag is used if present. Downloads the CamTorum map on first run.

**Setup:** Have a pickaxe and hammer available in inventory/bank (auto-withdrawn); optionally a gem bag. Zoom is auto-set. Configure options in the GUI.

**Features:**
- Power-mining mode (drops deposits/gems, no banking)
- Ignore-waterfalls toggle (skip wet rock switching)
- Automatic anvil processing of calcified deposits
- Gem bag open/empty handling
- Discord webhook notifications with termination pings
- State-machine walking between mine sites, blacksmith and bank
bigaussie-gemstone-crab-slayer-by-bigaus:
**What it does:** Fights the Gemstone Crab at Tal Teklan (Varlamore) for largely AFK combat XP, travelling there via Renu and attacking the north/east/south crab spawns while managing health, prayer and buffs. It supports melee and ranged setups and a wide range of combat automation and reporting.

**Requirements:** Members, Tal Teklan / Gemstone Crab access, appropriate combat gear (melee or ranged), and the relevant potions/food; Dharok's set, special-attack weapon, or bolts required for those optional modes; runes/items for the chosen Renu return method.

**Setup:** Equip combat gear and stock potions/food; select combat style and buffs in the GUI. For farm/birdhouse runs, prepare seeds, compost and run supplies as prompted by the GUI panels.

**Features:**
- Melee or Ranged mode
- Dharok's low-HP mode with target-HP setting
- Quick prayers, prayer flicking, and special attacks / dragon battleaxe
- Combat boosts with re-pot interval
- Per-skill stop-at-level goals (saved between sessions)
- World hopping at configurable intervals
- Integrated farm runs and birdhouse runs, bolt enchanting
- Discord webhooks (hourly/session) and on-screen UI overlay
bigaussie-ironwood-chopper-by-bigaussie:
**What it does:** Chops Ironwood trees at a fixed grove, waiting on each tree until XP stops or it depletes, then either banks the logs at a nearby bank chest or power-drops them. It weights tree selection (optionally toward trees with other players for Forestry) and can hop worlds on a timer.

**Requirements:** Members, Ironwood tree/grove access, the Woodcutting level and axe for Ironwood trees, and FIXED CLASSIC client mode (the script terminates otherwise). World hopping needs at least two worlds configured for the player.

**Setup:** Have an axe equipped or in inventory and start near the Ironwood grove in FIXED CLASSIC mode. Configure world-hopping, Forestry, power-cut, breaks and webhooks in the GUI.

**Features:**
- Bank logs or power-cut (drop) mode, keeping nests/clue scrolls
- Forestry mode: prefer trees with nearby players
- Weighted tree rotation biased toward the bank
- Timed world hopping with random variation
- Special-attack (spec weapon) usage when full
- Custom break interval/duration override
- Discord hourly reports and session summary; on-screen overlay
bigaussie-salt-and-basalt-miner-by-bigau:
**What it does:** Mines salts (Te, Efh, Urt) and Basalt in the Weiss underground salt mine, descending/ascending the stairs as needed, and can craft mined salts and basalt into Stony basalt and Icy basalt or note plain basalt via Snowflake. It mines toward user-set target counts and logs out when reached.

**Requirements:** Members, Weiss salt mine access (Making Friends with My Arm quest), and a pickaxe. Crafting basalt uses the mined salts (Te + Urt for Stony, Te + Efh for Icy).

**Setup:** Start with a pickaxe; enter desired target quantities for each salt/basalt type in the GUI. Zoom and mouse speed are auto-set.

**Features:**
- Mine-and-note Basalt-only mode via Snowflake
- Per-type target counts for Te, Efh and Urt salt
- Auto-crafting of Stony basalt and Icy basalt to target amounts
- Automatic stair descent/ascent between surface and mine
- Profit and XP/hr progress reporting
- Auto-logout and terminate when all targets are met
bigaussie-trouble-brewing-by-bigaussie:
**What it does:** Automates the Trouble Brewing minigame end-to-end, detecting your team, joining a crew, and repeatedly chopping the Scrapey tree then either fletching the logs (Scrapy Bark method) or filling buckets at the water pump to feed the hoppers, accumulating Pieces of Eight and handling the end-game reward interface. It reads the round timer, moves between the lower/upper house levels, and manages tools from the workbench.

**Requirements:** Members' world with access to the Trouble Brewing minigame (Mos Le'Harmless, Cabin Fever quest), a valid woodcutting axe, standard spellbook not required; internet access for optional Discord webhooks.

**Setup:** Start at or near the Trouble Brewing lobby with an axe equipped or in inventory; drop any stray items; the script grabs a knife or buckets from the in-game workbench itself.

**Features:**
- Team selector (Red, Blue, or Random)
- Method selector: Scrapy Bark (fletch logs) or Buckets (fill water)
- Power Fletch option (drop bark while fletching)
- Automatic team/side detection by player position
- Teleports back to the minigame via the transport handler
- Discord webhook notifications with optional user-ID ping
- Ping on script termination/crash
- Per-account settings saved to an INI file
bird-catcher-by-bootje:
**What it does:** Hunts birds using bird snares at a chosen hunting spot, walking to a base tile, laying a snare, watching the trap, then checking/dismantling and re-laying it while dropping the bones and raw bird meat produced. Tracks actions, XP, and profit.

**Requirements:** Members' world and the Hunter level appropriate for the selected bird (copper longtail, cerulean twitch, or tropical wagtail), at least one Bird snare, some free inventory space.

**Setup:** Have a Bird snare in the inventory with fewer than 25 items used (needs free space); start at or near the selected hunting area.

**Features:**
- Location/bird selector: Piscatoris, Kourend, or Isle of Souls (Copper longtail), Rellekka lower level (Cerulean twitch), Feldip Hunter area (Tropical wagtail)
- Auto-drops Bones and Raw bird meat
- Trap-locating via color search after laying
- Account manager, antiban, and bank settings panels
booties-telealcher-by-bootje:
**What it does:** Trains Magic using the tele-alch method: it casts High Alchemy on a chosen item and then immediately casts Camelot Teleport in a loop, tracking XP and computing profit after subtracting law-rune cost. Includes a 30-second no-XP safety net that terminates the script.

**Requirements:** Standard spellbook, runes for High Alchemy (nature runes) and Camelot Teleport (law/air runes), and an item to alch; magic level high enough for both spells.

**Setup:** Place the alch item and the required runes in the inventory before starting; select the alch item in the alchemy panel.

**Features:**
- Alchemy panel to choose the item to alch (high alch)
- Automatic Camelot Teleport between alch casts
- Profit tracking net of law-rune cost
- 30-second safety net that stops if no XP is gained
credentials-helper-by-baconadors:
**What it does:** A utility (not a bot) that opens a GUI for entering and saving account login credentials into the launcher's account manager, so accounts can be added or updated. It downloads an instructional background image and displays guidance for the different account types.

**Requirements:** Not specified in code (utility tool; no in-game requirements).

**Setup:** No in-game preparation; just fill in account username, password, and optional bank pin/worlds, then click Add/Update Account.

**Features:**
- Account manager for adding/updating credentials
- On-screen instructions distinguishing Jagex accounts vs legacy/non-Jagex accounts
- Optional bank pin and preferred worlds fields
- Downloads and shows a help image in the window background
deetss-teleporter-by-deetss:
**What it does:** Repeatedly casts a single user-selected standard-spellbook teleport spell to train Magic, dismissing any teleport warning dialogs and waiting for each cast to complete. Verifies the standard spellbook on startup.

**Requirements:** Standard spellbook, and the runes/level for whichever teleport spell is chosen.

**Setup:** Stock the inventory with the runes for the selected teleport spell before starting.

**Features:**
- Teleport-spell selector auto-populated with all standard teleport spells (excluding Home)
- Handles teleport confirmation/warning prompts
- Antiban manager and account manager panels
godtier-quarry-miner-rewritten-by-godtie:
**What it does:** Mines sandstone and/or granite at the Bandit Camp quarry, either depositing sandstone into the grinder (tracking buckets of sand) or powermining and dropping granite, in a state-machine loop with special-attack use and level-up handling. Supports two heat-survival styles and logs out on low HP.

**Requirements:** Members' world with desert quarry access and Mining level; for the CoW/DA4 style a Circlet of Water or Desert amulet 4; for the Humidify style the Lunar spellbook plus waterskins.

**Setup:** Equip a Circlet of Water or Desert amulet 4 (CoW style), or carry waterskins and be on the Lunar spellbook (Humidify style); start with a mostly empty inventory near the quarry.

**Features:**
- Mining style selector: CoW/DA4 vs Humidify (auto-refills waterskins)
- Rock selector: Sandstone (grinder), Granite (powermine), or Both
- Location selector: South Quarry or North Quarry
- Optional sand-bucket collection limit with editable target that stops the script
- Reads current grinder bucket count from Drew
- Logs out at 50% HP for safety and refills on HP drops
- Saves selected options to a JSON config
hidecrafter-by-taze:
**What it does:** Crafts dragonhide armour at a bank by withdrawing dragon leather (and thread/needle), using the needle on leather, selecting the make option for the chosen item, then depositing finished products in a loop with profit tracking. Handles collection-box retrieval and stops when supplies run out.

**Requirements:** Members' world, Crafting level for the chosen dragonhide item, and a stock of the selected dragon leather (plus thread if using a normal needle) in the bank.

**Setup:** Start at a bank with dragon leather of the chosen color, a needle (normal or costume), and thread (for normal needle) available; the script withdraws supplies itself.

**Features:**
- Leather color selector: Green, Blue, Red, Black
- Armour type selector: Vambraces, Chaps, Body
- Needle type selector: NORMAL or COSTUME (costume needs no thread)
- Optimizes needle/leather slot proximity for faster crafting
- Collection-box handling when the bank runs empty
- Profit and XP tracking per action
hunter-by-aetherdescent:
**What it does:** Automates hunting in Varlamore's Hunters' Guild across multiple modes, including pitfall trapping Sunlight/Moonlight antelopes, netting Moonlight/Sunlight moths, Ruby harvest and Crimson swift butterflies, bird snaring, completing hunter Rumours, and making mixes. Optionally runs birdhouse runs on Fossil Island and full herb/allotment/flower/tree farm runs as background chores.

**Requirements:** Membership, Varlamore and Hunters' Guild access, Fossil Island access (for birdhouse chore, via Digsite pendant), appropriate Hunter/Fletching/Firemaking/Farming levels for the chosen activity, quest access to the various farming patches and teleports used

**Setup:** Withdraw the tools the chosen mode needs (teasing stick, knife/chisel, bird snares, butterfly jars, food, logs, coins), optionally a quetzal whistle (basic/enhanced/perfected) and a small/large meat pouch; for chores prepare seeds, compost, logs, teleport items (Digsite pendant, farming teleports/cape, Ardougne cloak, dueling ring, spirit tree, basalts) in the bank

**Features:**
- Mode selector (Rumours, Sunlight/Moonlight Antelope, Moonlight/Sunlight Moth, Ruby Harvest, Crimson Swift, Make Mixes)
- Quetzal whistle tier and meat pouch selection for fast banking/travel
- Per-animal handling of antlers (fletch into bolts / bank / drop) and meat (bank / drop)
- Optional bury big bones, bank moonlight moths, and Outer Fortis quetzal site toggles
- Configurable food type and withdraw amount
- Optional birdhouse runs with selectable log type and start-timer
- Optional multi-region herb/flower/allotment/fruit/hops farm runs with seed selection
- Break/sleep scheduling and settings saved to an INI config
lava-runecrafter-free-by-bootje:
**What it does:** Crafts lava runes by combining earth runes with pure essence at the Fire Altar, then teleports to Castle Wars via a ring of dueling to bank and restock in a repeating loop.

**Requirements:** Membership (ring of dueling and binding necklace are members items), Runecrafting level for lava runes (23+), access to the Fire Altar area

**Setup:** Bank stocked with pure essence, earth runes, earth talisman, binding necklace, rings of dueling(8), and stamina potions; equip/keep a ring of dueling and binding necklace for teleporting and combining runes

**Features:**
- Optional stamina potion use to maintain run energy
- Optional F-key game-tab switching
- Automatic jewelry management (re-equips binding necklace and swaps depleted dueling rings)
- Detangle/recovery routine that teleports back via Clan Wars if lost
- Account manager integration
lazy-firemaker-by-aetherdescent:
**What it does:** Burns logs for Firemaking by lighting fires on a fixed spot next to a bank chest, working through a user-defined queue of log jobs and restocking from the bank between inventories.

**Requirements:** Firemaking level appropriate for each selected log type

**Setup:** Logs for the chosen job(s) and a tinderbox stored in the bank, standing at the supported bank-chest firemaking location

**Features:**
- Task list where you add multiple log-type + amount jobs
- Log type dropdown (Logs through Redwood)
- Break-after / break-for scheduling
- Link to the OSRS Firemaking calculator for planning log counts
- World-hopping failsafe when a fire can't be started
- Auto-withdraws its own tinderbox and logs out when jobs finish
- Progress reporting of XP and actions per hour
seagull-slayer-by-bootje:
**What it does:** Trains melee combat by repeatedly attacking Seagulls with unarmed styles, cycling through combat styles for balanced stat training over a timed session.

**Requirements:** Access to a coastal Seagull spawn area (Port Piscarilius region)

**Setup:** Stand near seagulls unarmed (nothing wielded so kick/punch/block styles are available); no bank items needed

**Features:**
- Toggle to train all combat styles or a single style
- Configurable minutes spent per style
- Cycles Kick / Punch / Block attack styles
- Automatically re-attacks the nearest seagull when out of combat
- Account manager integration
settings-searcher-by-canadianjames:
**What it does:** A one-time setup/utility script that verifies and corrects in-game client settings (shift-drop, esc-to-close, middle-mouse camera, roofs, tint, XP bar layout, spell filters, brightness, fixed client mode) so other WaspLib scripts run correctly. It also inspects the RuneLite client/profile and can restart RuneLite with the Wasp profile.

**Requirements:** Must be logged in when run; RuneLite (for the client/plugin checks and profile restart)

**Setup:** No inventory or bank preparation; simply run while logged into the game

**Features:**
- Toggles recommended interface settings on and the world switcher off
- Configures XP bar setup (position, size, counter, colour, etc.) and verifies XP reading
- Disables magic spellbook filtering
- Sets max brightness and switches client to Fixed mode
- Detects RuneLite GPU plugin and warns to disable it
- Can restart RuneLite using the installed Wasp profile
slackys-object-creator-and-aca-by-slacky:
**What it does:** A developer GUI utility (AutoObjectAid) for building color-based object finders: you capture the game client image, paint over a target object, and it auto-generates CTS2 colors via ACA/kappa-sigma color clipping plus a full TRSComplexObject definition you can paste into your own Simba scripts. It is a scripting/coding aid, not an in-game bot.

**Requirements:** Not specified in code (offline developer tool; needs only the RuneScape client image, no levels/items/membership).

**Setup:** Have the RuneScape client open and visible with the object you want to model on screen so the tool can capture and redraw its image.

**Features:**
- Paint-brush area selection with adjustable brush size, plus an eraser tool
- Automatic color analysis (ACA) with adjustable kappa-sigma clipping
- Approved-color and trash-color lists with move-to-trash and color reduction
- Finder tuning: cluster/split, erode, grow, size, area, short/long side, density, accepted deviation
- Per-color 'essential' flag
- Minimap/world-map position filtering (Map and Dynamic-select options)
- Live-test overlay to preview matches on the client
- Generate object code and copy-ACA / copy-object output
squirkin-minigame-by-bigaussie:
**What it does:** Farms Summer sq'irks in the Sorceress's Garden minigame: teleports in via the Apprentice, enters the summer garden, grinds sq'irks into sq'irk juice with a pestle and mortar, hands juice to Osman/Selim for Thieving XP, and banks for more beer glasses, looping continuously.

**Requirements:** Thieving level for the summer garden (65+), access to Al Kharid/Sorceress's Garden, and the Apprentice teleport unlocked. Members. Energy/stamina consumables optional for the run-heavy maze.

**Setup:** Bank a pestle and mortar and beer glasses; optionally keep Stamina potions on the top row of the bank if using energy items. Start in the Al Kharid area near the Apprentice.

**Features:**
- State machine: teleport to garden, enter, mine/pick sq'irks, make juice, hand in, bank, exit
- Configurable energy points to consume, with a custom consumable handler that avoids eating sq'irks or dropping beer glasses
- Auto-withdraws pestle/mortar, energy items, and the right number of beer glasses
- Drops unwanted random-event items automatically
- Downloads the Sorceress's Garden map on first run
- Live report estimating XP (3000 per juice) and XP/hour
students-ammo-fletcher-by-student:
**What it does:** Fletches ranged ammunition by using an unfinished component (dart tips, unfinished bolts, or unfinished broad bolts) together with feathers and repeatedly selecting the make option until materials run out.

**Requirements:** Fletching level appropriate to the chosen ammo; feathers and the selected tips/unfinished bolts in the inventory; broad bolts additionally need the usual Slayer/broad-ammo unlock; members for most tiers.

**Setup:** Fill the inventory with feathers plus the selected unfinished ammo/tips, then pick the ammo type in the GUI dropdown. No banking is performed; the script stops when out of ingredients or feathers.

**Features:**
- GUI dropdown to select ammo type (bronze-to-dragon dart tips, amethyst, bronze-to-dragon unfinished bolts, broad bolts)
- Automatic Make-interface / make-string handling
- Level-up handling
- Auto-stops when out of tips or feathers
- Account manager, antiban, bank and API/WaspLib settings tabs
students-ore-buyer-by-student:
**What it does:** Buys a selected ore from Ordan's ore shop at the Blast Furnace, banks the ore (emptying a coal bag if used), and hops worlds when the shop stock is low or overstocked, while estimating profit.

**Requirements:** Coins in inventory; Blast Furnace access (membership); client in fixed-classic mode. Optionally a coal bag in inventory.

**Setup:** Start at the Blast Furnace near Ordan and the bank chest with coins in the inventory and the client in fixed mode; configure ore, minimum shop stock, total buy limit, and breaks/sleeps in the GUI.

**Features:**
- GUI ore selector (gold, silver, tin, copper, mithril, iron, coal)
- Minimum-shop-stock and total-buy-limit settings
- World hopping when stock is low or overstocked
- Coal bag fill/empty support
- Optional stamina/energy restoring
- Break and sleep scheduling
- Profit estimation from live item prices
students-volcanic-ash-miner-by-student:
**What it does:** Mines volcanic ash from the four ash piles on Fossil Island, dropping soda ash and uncut gems, and hops worlds if another player crashes the spot.

**Requirements:** Mining level for volcanic ash (the script uses slower timing at level 50 or below); Fossil Island access (Bone Voyage quest); members.

**Setup:** Stand at the volcanic ash area on Fossil Island near the piles (start tile) with a mostly empty inventory; set run time, collect count, and breaks/sleeps in the GUI.

**Features:**
- Mines four ash piles in sequence then returns to the start tile
- Auto-drops soda ash and uncut sapphire/emerald/ruby/diamond
- Player-crash detection with world hopping
- Collect-count limit and maximum runtime
- Adjusts mining wait time based on mining level
- Break and sleep scheduling
students-wines-by-student:
**What it does:** Makes unfermented wine (regular or Zamorak) by withdrawing jugs of water and grapes from the bank and combining them through the Make interface, then banking and repeating.

**Requirements:** Cooking level 35+ for wine; jugs of water and grapes (or Zamorak's grapes) stocked in the bank; client in fixed-classic mode; members for Zamorak's grapes.

**Setup:** Start at a bank with jugs of water and the chosen grapes available; client in fixed mode; select grape type, wine count, run time, and breaks/sleeps in the GUI (settings persist to an INI file).

**Features:**
- GUI dropdown for grape type (Grapes / Zamorak's grapes)
- Total wine count and run-for limits
- Settings persisted between runs via INI file
- Bank withdraw/mix/deposit loop
- Level-up handling
- Break and sleep scheduling
superheater-by-taze:
**What it does:** Casts the Superheat Item spell on ores withdrawn from the bank to smelt bars (bronze through runite), deposits the bars, and repeats; supports a coal bag for coal-heavy bars.

**Requirements:** Magic level 43+ for Superheat Item (plus the smithing level for higher bars); nature runes and a fire source (fire runes/staff); the relevant ores stocked in the bank; client in fixed-classic mode; members.

**Setup:** Start with an empty inventory at a bank where the ores and runes are visible without scrolling; client in fixed mode. Select bar type and optionally enable the coal bag (for Steel/Mithril/Adamantite/Runite).

**Features:**
- GUI bar-type selector (Bronze to Runite)
- Coal bag support for coal-based bars
- Configurable cast rune (Nature; Fire for a Bryophyta staff)
- Cast-failure threshold before re-banking
- Mouse speed override
- Collect-box handling
- Auto max brightness and fixed-mode enforcement
thief-aio-portroberts-by-kriptic:
**What it does:** Thieves from the stalls at Port Robert, prioritizing higher-value stalls (cannonballs, ore, gems, spice, silver, fish, fur, silk, vegetables) based on your thieving level, and automatically escapes from jail and banks/drops loot as the inventory fills. It tick-manipulates its rob timing and can intentionally get jailed to mimic human behavior.

**Requirements:** Access to Port Robert; thieving level appropriate to the chosen stall tier (options span level 2 to 87).

**Setup:** Start at Port Robert's stall area; the script banks (via deposit box) when the inventory holds 8-12 non-dropped items and recovers from jail on its own. Use the drop/keep list to mark which loot to keep vs shift-drop.

**Features:**
- Stall selector with per-level tiers plus an 'Auto: Best XP' mode
- Configurable world-hop interval (15/30/45/60 min)
- Intentional jail rate (0-5%) and jail interval to look human
- Automatic jail escape and re-sync to the guard's timing
- Keep/Drop item list manager (green = keep and bank, red = drop)
- Breaks and sleep antiban with editable timings
- Max actions / max runtime stop conditions
wasp-aerial-fisher-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Performs aerial fishing on Molch Island, catching the fishing spots (Bluegill, Common tench, Mottled eel, Greater siren) with a cormorant, then either cuts the caught fish with a knife or shift-drops them to keep fishing. Optionally sips a Hunter potion to boost.

**Requirements:** Aerial fishing access with a Cormorant's glove equipped, fishing/hunter levels for the spots, membership, and starting bait (King worm or Fish offcuts).

**Setup:** Equip the Cormorant's glove and gather your own starting bait (buy fish chunks at the GE or gather worms on the island); carry a Knife if you want cut-fish mode, and a Hunter potion if you want the boost.

**Features:**
- Cut-fish toggle (cut with knife vs drop)
- Automatic Hunter potion boosting when enabled
- Standard antiban, WaspLib settings and API stats panels
wasp-agility-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Runs a chosen rooftop/agility course end to end, completing each obstacle in order, picking up Marks of Grace along the route, and optionally casting High Alchemy on a carried item while training. Supports courses from Draynor up to Ardougne.

**Requirements:** Agility level for the selected course (Draynor 1, Al Kharid 20, Varrock 30, Canifis 40, Ape Atoll 48, Falador 50, Seers/Camelot 60, Pollnivneach 70, Rellekka 80, Ardougne 90); membership for most courses; nature runes plus an alchable item if High Alchemy is enabled.

**Setup:** Start at (or near) the selected course; for High Alchemy have nature runes and the alch item configured via the alchemy panel. Camelot option uses the Seers' course with a teleport.

**Features:**
- Course selector (11 courses)
- High Alchemy toggle with dedicated alchemy item panel
- Automatic Mark of Grace pickup per course
- Antiban, WaspLib settings and API stats panels
wasp-alcher-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Casts High or Low Alchemy repeatedly on an item, withdrawing nature runes and coins from the bank (and collecting from the collection box) as needed, and can walk itself to the Grand Exchange in Varrock to alch there. Handles the alchemy warning dialog by setting a value threshold.

**Requirements:** Standard spellbook, Magic 55 for High Alchemy (21 for Low), nature runes, coins, and an item to alch in the bank/inventory.

**Setup:** Be on the standard spellbook with nature runes and coins bankable; keep the alch item available. Walk-to-GE option only supports the Varrock GE.

**Features:**
- High vs Low alchemy spell selector
- 'Prevent loss' option to avoid alching at a loss
- Walk-to-GE (Varrock) toggle
- Custom item field and collection-box handling
wasp-anvil-smither-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Smiths a chosen item at an anvil, banking to withdraw bars and a hammer, walking to the anvil, and making the item in bulk before depositing the products. Works at the Varrock or Prifddinas anvil and tracks profit per action.

**Requirements:** Smithing level for the chosen metal/item combo, the correct bars in the bank, a Hammer (or wieldable Imcando hammer); membership for the Prifddinas option.

**Setup:** Have the required bars and a hammer in the bank near the selected anvil (Varrock or Prifddinas); the script equips the Imcando hammer if present.

**Features:**
- Metal-type selector (Bronze, Iron, Steel, Mithril, Adamant, Rune)
- Item-type selector (28 items from dagger to platebody)
- Bank/location selector (Varrock or Prifddinas)
- Auto bar-count and multiplier handling per item (darts, bolts, arrows, etc.)
- Collection-box handling and run-energy enabling
- Remembers last metal/item/location via config
wasp-ardougne-knights-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Pickpockets the Knight of Ardougne, opening coin pouches when full, keeping a Dodgy necklace equipped, eating food when HP drops, and banking to restock necklaces and food. Detects and applies rogue equipment for higher loot and can maintain the Shadow Veil spell.

**Requirements:** Thieving 55 to pickpocket knights, access to Ardougne (Ardougne Achievement Diary tiers increase coin-pouch capacity), membership; optional Shadow Veil (Arceuus) with a lava staff and cosmic runes.

**Setup:** Bank near the Ardougne knight with Dodgy necklaces and food; optionally bring full Rogue equipment (mask/top/trousers/gloves/boots), a Thieving cape, and a lava battlestaff for Shadow Veil.

**Features:**
- Configurable necklace amount, food amount and minimum-health heal threshold
- Auto-detects and equips rogue gear for a loot multiplier
- Optional Shadow Veil upkeep for higher pickpocket success
- Dodgy necklace tracking/re-equip and coin-pouch auto-opening
- Handles going downstairs, chat-filter setup and low-HP eating
- Config remembers necklace/food/health settings
wasp-astral-runecrafter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Crafts Astral runes at the Moon Clan altar on Lunar Isle, teleporting between the bank and altar with the Moonclan teleport, filling essence pouches, and repairing broken pouches via NPC Contact with the Dark Mage. It runs a full state-machine loop of banking, filling pouches, crafting, and storing runes in a rune pouch.

**Requirements:** 40+ Runecrafting (higher levels unlock larger pouches: 25+ medium, 50+ large, 75+ giant), Lunar spellbook with Moonclan/Tele Group Moonclan and NPC Contact, Lunar Diary / Dream Mentor / Lunar quest access to Lunar Isle, membership, law and cosmic runes for teleports and contact

**Setup:** Start with the essence pouches your level can use plus a rune pouch containing law runes, cosmics and 2 astrals, a Dust battlestaff equipped, pure essence (or Daeyalt essence) in the bank; optionally stamina potions and food

**Features:**
- Bank mode selector: Western booth only, Eastern booth only, or All booths (also Dream Mentor seal variants)
- Optional stamina potion use for run energy
- Supports Colossal, Giant, Large, Medium and Small pouches with level-based capacity
- Auto-detects Daeyalt essence, falling back to pure essence
- Auto NPC Contact + Dark Mage repair for degraded pouches
- Works with Rune pouch, Divine rune pouch, or raw astral/law runes
- Profit tracking for astrals, essence, teleports and contact costs
- Collect box support when the bank runs out of essence
wasp-blast-furnace-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Runs the Blast Furnace minigame, banking ore (and coal) at the bank chest, loading it onto the conveyor belt, collecting finished bars from the dispenser, and topping up the coffer with coins to pay the furnace fee. It tracks loaded coal against the coal ratio to keep the melting pot correctly stocked.

**Requirements:** Membership, a Blast Furnace world, coins for the furnace fee (half fee under level 60 Smithing), enough ore/coal in the bank; Goldsmith gauntlets or Ice gloves for gold bars; Smithing cape/Max cape gives a larger coal bag

**Setup:** Start inside a Blast Furnace world with ore and coins in the bank; bring a coal bag and/or energy potions if those options are enabled; gold-bar mode needs goldsmith or ice gloves

**Features:**
- Bar type selector: Bronze, Iron, Silver, Steel, Gold, Mithril, Adamantite, Runite
- Optional coal bag support (auto-disabled for bar types that don't need coal)
- Optional energy/stamina potion boosts
- Automatic coffer refilling and fee payment via money-count reading
- Coal-ratio pot syncing and resync logic to avoid over/under-loading
- Auto-swaps goldsmith and ice gloves for gold bars
- Warns and stops if not in a Blast Furnace world
wasp-builder-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Trains Construction in a player-owned house by repeatedly building and removing a chosen piece of furniture, restocking planks either from Phials (noted planks) or a demon butler/servant. It enters build mode, builds, destroys, and refetches planks in a loop.

**Requirements:** Membership, a player-owned house with the correct furniture room, Construction level for the chosen furniture, a hammer and saw, coins; Phials method needs noted planks; butler method needs a servant with a money bag and a bell-pull

**Setup:** Bring hammer, saw, coins and (Phials mode) noted planks, optionally a plank sack; house layout must match the shown reference image with only the relevant room visible; larder in the NE kitchen, cape mount in the SE hall; butler mode needs a stocked servant's money bag and a pre-requested 24-plank fetch

**Features:**
- Restock method selector: Phials or Demon butler
- Furniture selector: Regular larder, Oak larder, Myth cape mount, Mahogany table
- Optional plank sack support for larger plank batches
- Automatic bank pin entry when entering the house
- Downloads and displays reference house-layout images in the GUI
- Profit/plank-cost tracking per build
wasp-charter-superglass-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Makes glass with the Lunar Superglass Make spell by buying buckets of sand and soda ash/seaweed from charter ship Trader Crewmembers, then blows the molten glass into a chosen glass product and sells it back. It world-hops when a shop is sold out.

**Requirements:** Lunar spellbook with Superglass Make (77 Magic / Lunar Diary), membership, Crafting level appropriate to the chosen glass item, coins for buying materials, access to charter ship traders

**Setup:** Be on the lunar spellbook near a charter ship trader with a glassblowing pipe and coins; the script buys sand and soda ash/seaweed itself

**Features:**
- Glass item selector: Beer glass, Candle lantern, Oil lamp, Vial, Fishbowl, Unpowered orb, Lantern lens, Light orb, or Best available
- 'Best available' auto-picks the highest product for your Crafting level
- Configurable glass amount (materials bought per trip)
- Buys glassblowing pipe, sand and ash from the trader shop
- World hopping across a large member-worlds list when shops empty
- Drops light orbs instead of selling (no shop value)
wasp-chompy-hunter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Hunts Chompy birds in the swamp south of Castle Wars by filling ogre bellows at swamp bubbles, inflating swamp toads into bloated toads, placing them as bait, and shooting/plucking the chompy birds that appear. It loops fill-bellows, fill-toad, place-toad, attack.

**Requirements:** Membership, Big Chompy Bird Hunting quest, an ogre bow with ogre/brutal arrows equipped, ogre bellows; range level for chompy birds

**Setup:** Start south of Castle Wars in the middle pools near the Smoke Dungeon, with ogre bellows in the inventory and an ogre bow and arrows equipped

**Features:**
- Automatic bellows filling at swamp bubbles
- Inflates swamp toads and places bloated toad bait
- Moves off swamp/NPC tiles before placing toads
- Detects combat and feather gain to confirm kills
- Deliberately minimal 'get the job done' script (author suggests babysitting)
wasp-combat-by-torwent:
**What it does:** A general slayer/combat killer that walks to a chosen monster's location, fights it with auto-retaliate, manages health/prayer thresholds, loots by value, and can high-alch loot. It can stop automatically when a slayer task is completed.

**Requirements:** Membership and quest/area access for many listed dungeons, combat stats suited to the chosen monster; slayer level for slayer-only monsters; optional runes for High Level Alchemy; teleport items (Rada's blessing, Karamja gloves, Varrock teleport) for the task-finish routine

**Setup:** Start at or near the selected monster's location with appropriate weapon, armour, food, prayer potions and looting supplies; the script auto-selects map chunks per monster

**Features:**
- Large monster selector (~55 monsters from cows to Hydras, Gargoyles, Abyssal demons, etc.)
- Auto location/chunk selection with multiple location options per monster
- Configurable minimum health and prayer thresholds (randomized per player)
- Optional 'Stop after slayer task' with slayer helm/bracelet checks
- Optional starting-tile safe spot for ranged/safe killing
- Loot manager with configurable loot value threshold
- Optional High Level Alchemy of loot
- Screenshots superior slayer foes and task completion
wasp-cooker-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Banks raw food, cooks it at a fixed cooking spot (Hosidius clay oven, Rogues' Den fire, or Myths' Guild range), and banks the cooked and burnt results in a loop. It tracks cooked-vs-raw profit per action.

**Requirements:** Cooking level appropriate to the chosen food, membership for member locations/foods, raw food stocked in the bank; Client of Kourend / Myths' Guild access for those locations

**Setup:** Start near the chosen cooking location with raw food in the bank; select the food type (or enter a custom exact item name)

**Features:**
- Location selector: Hosidius kitchen, Rogues' Den, or Myths' Guild
- Food selector covering ~24 raw foods plus a 'Custom' exact-name option
- Uses the near-burn-free Hosidius oven for low burn rates
- Handles cooked and burnt product depositing
- Collect box support when the bank runs dry
- Profit tracking based on cooked vs raw price
wasp-craft-guild-tanner-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Tans hides into leather at the Crafting Guild tanner, banking downstairs, climbing up to the tanner NPC with hides and coins, tanning, and returning to bank the leather. It repeats the climb-tan-bank cycle and tracks profit per hide.

**Requirements:** 40 Crafting and a brown apron (or achievement diary) for Crafting Guild access, membership for member hides, coins for tanning fees, hides stocked in the bank

**Setup:** Start in/near the Crafting Guild with hides and coins in the bank; select which hide/leather type to tan

**Features:**
- Hide selector: Soft, Hard, Swamp snake, Snake, Green/Blue/Red/Black dragon leather
- Automatic staircase up/down navigation between bank and tanner
- Per-hide tan-fee and profit tracking
- Bank pin handling
- Collect box support for restocking hides
- Pre-hovering of stairs/bank objects for faster cycles
wasp-enchanter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Casts the appropriate enchant-jewellery spell (Lvl 1-7 Enchant) on rings, necklaces, bracelets or amulets at a bank, then banks the enchanted product and repeats. Restocks from the bank and can pull supplies from a collect box when the bank runs dry.

**Requirements:** Magic level sufficient for the chosen enchant spell, cosmic runes plus the elemental runes for that enchant tier, and a stock of the unenchanted jewellery matching the selected gem/jewel type.

**Setup:** Bank stocked with cosmic runes and the chosen regular jewellery; start at the configured bank location (default Grand Exchange). No special equipment required.

**Features:**
- Gem selector (Opal through Zenyte) and jewel-type selector (Ring/Necklace/Bracelet/Amulet)
- Automatically maps gem+jewel choice to the correct enchant spell level and finished item
- Configurable bank location
- Collect-box support to draw supplies when the bank is empty
- Profit tracking based on live item prices
- Antiban and account-manager integration
wasp-fletcher-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Fletches logs into arrow shafts, javelin shafts, (un)strung shortbows/longbows, crossbow stocks or shields at a bank using a knife (or strings unstrung bows with bow string), then banks the products. Uses the Make interface and restocks from bank or collect box.

**Requirements:** Fletching level appropriate for the chosen log/item, a knife (or bow string for stringing bows), and a supply of the selected log type.

**Setup:** Bank stocked with the chosen logs and a knife (or bow string); start at the configured bank (default Grand Exchange).

**Features:**
- Log selector: Regular, Oak, Willow, Maple, Yew, Magic, Redwood
- Item selector: Arrow shaft, Javelin shaft, Shortbow (u), Shortbow, Stock, Longbow (u), Longbow, Shield
- Handles both cutting logs and stringing unstrung bows automatically
- Configurable bank location
- Collect-box support when the bank is empty
- Withdraws finished products (noted) on termination for script chaining
- Profit tracking and antiban integration
wasp-gem-cutter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Uses a chisel to cut uncut gems into cut gems, and/or fletches cut gems into bolt tips, at a bank, then banks the results. Supports three modes and restocks from bank or collect box.

**Requirements:** Crafting level for the chosen gem (and Fletching level when making bolt tips), a chisel, and a stock of uncut (or cut) gems of the selected type.

**Setup:** Bank stocked with a chisel and the chosen uncut/cut gems; start at the configured bank (default Grand Exchange).

**Features:**
- Gem selector: Opal, Jade, Red Topaz, Sapphire, Emerald, Ruby, Diamond, Dragonstone, Onyx
- Mode selector: Uncut > Cut, Uncut > Bolt tips, Cut > Bolt tips
- Handles crushed-gem byproducts by depositing them
- Configurable bank location
- Collect-box support when the bank is empty
- Profit tracking (accounts for bolt-tip yield of 12x, doubled for Onyx)
wasp-glassblower-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Uses a glassblowing pipe on molten glass to blow glass items (beer glass, lanterns, oil lamps, vials, fishbowls, orbs, lantern lenses, light orbs) at a bank, then banks the products. Can auto-pick the best item for the player's current Crafting level.

**Requirements:** Crafting level appropriate for the chosen glass item, a glassblowing pipe, and a supply of molten glass.

**Setup:** Bank stocked with a glassblowing pipe and molten glass; start at the configured bank (default Grand Exchange).

**Features:**
- Glass item selector plus a "Best available" option that tracks Crafting level and switches products automatically on level-ups
- Configurable bank location
- Collect-box support when the bank is empty
- Withdraws finished products (noted) on termination for script chaining
- Profit tracking and antiban integration
wasp-herblore-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Mixes a wide range of Herblore potions (from unfinished potions or full multi-ingredient recipes) at a bank by combining a vial/base with ingredients, then banks the finished potions. Restocks from bank or collect box.

**Requirements:** Herblore level for the chosen potion, plus the recipe's base (vial of water/blood, unfinished potion, coconut milk, etc.) and ingredient(s); some recipes are members-only.

**Setup:** Bank stocked with the correct base and ingredients for the selected potion; start at the configured bank (default Grand Exchange). Optionally equip an Amulet of chemistry, Herblore cape, or Prescription goggles for bonuses.

**Features:**
- Large potion selector covering unfinished potions, standard potions, divine/extended/anti-venom variants, and super combat
- Optional Necklace (Amulet) of chemistry support with charge tracking for 4-dose bonus outputs
- Detects equipped Herblore cape (grimy-herb cleaning) and Prescription goggles
- Configurable bank location
- Collect-box support when the bank is empty
- Profit tracking distinguishing 3-dose and 4-dose outputs
wasp-jewelry-smelter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Smelts silver or gold bars (optionally set with a gem) at a furnace into jewellery such as tiaras, holy/unholy symbols, silver bolts, rings, necklaces, bracelets and amulets using the appropriate mould, then banks the products.

**Requirements:** Crafting level for the chosen item, the correct mould, silver/gold bars, and gems if making gem-set jewellery; furnace/bank access at the selected town.

**Setup:** Bank stocked with bars, the matching mould, and any gems; start near the chosen furnace location. Silver items and tiaras are auto-set to the correct metal.

**Features:**
- Furnace/location selector: Edgeville, Falador, Prifddinas
- Jewel-type selector: Tiara, Holy symbol, Unholy symbol, Bolts, Ring, Necklace, Bracelet, Amulet
- Stone selector (None + Opal through Zenyte) and Silver/Gold bar selector, with GUI logic enabling/forcing valid combinations
- Uses craft/silver/make screens as appropriate per item
- Collect-box support when the bank is empty
- Profit tracking and antiban integration
wasp-kourend-runecrafter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Runecrafts blood or soul runes in Arceuus (Kourend): mines dense essence, venerates it at the Dark Altar, chisels dark essence blocks into fragments, then binds them at the Blood or Soul Altar, looping continuously. Uses agility shortcuts where the player qualifies.

**Requirements:** Runecrafting/Crafting/Mining levels for the activity, a chisel (mandatory), Arceuus favour/quest access to the altars; higher Agility (49/52/69/73) unlocks faster shortcuts. Members-only area.

**Setup:** Start in the Kourend runecrafting region with a chisel in the inventory; optionally carry a Blood essence for activation. No banking is performed.

**Features:**
- Rune selector: Blood runes or Soul runes
- Agility-gated shortcut usage for the quarry and altar (auto-detected from Agility level)
- Blood essence activation support with profit adjustment
- Chisels essence blocks into fragments (caps around 108) and binds runes at the altar
- Breaks essence blocks during walking antiban to save time
- Rune-count-based profit tracking
wasp-kraken-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Kills the Kraken boss in its private instanced cove: travels there via fairy ring, pays to enter the crevice, wakes and attacks the Kraken with magic (Sang staff or blood spells), eats/drinks to survive, boosts with an imbued heart, and loots drops. Banks loot and restocks food when routed through Varrock.

**Requirements:** Magic and Hitpoints levels for boss combat, a Kraken slayer task/access to the cove, fishing explosives (required to wake the Kraken), Dramen/Lunar staff and fairy-ring access, and food; members-only. Ancient spellbook needed for blood-spell healing.

**Setup:** Bring a magic attack setup (Sang staff highly recommended since it does not bank between kills), food, prayer potions, fishing explosives, and quick-prayers configured for magic; optionally an imbued/saturated heart. Start with fairy-ring/teleport access.

**Features:**
- Fairy-ring travel (AKQ) and crevice payment handling
- Blood-spell healing detection (Barrage/Blitz/Burst/Rush) or food-based survival
- Quick-prayer auto-setup favouring Augury/Mystic prayers; prayer-potion drinking
- Imbued/Saturated heart magic boosting on a timer
- Loot handler with configurable loot manager; high-alchemy or drop of junk
- Auto-banks loot and withdraws food via Varrock teleport when out of food
- Stops on slayer-task completion; combat bracelet re-equipping
wasp-miner-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Mines a chosen rock type (clay through runite plus amethyst), then either drops the ore in place at a power-mining spot or banks it at a supported location, using a state machine that walks to the rocks, mines, waits for the animation/XP, and handles level-ups and rock-golem specs. It also uses a mining special-attack weapon at 100% spec.

**Requirements:** Membership required for some rocks/locations (P2P Mining Guild, Crafting Guild, amethyst); appropriate Mining level for the selected ore; access to the chosen area (Varrock east/west, Crafting Guild, Mining Guild, or a power-mine spot). Not specified in code beyond that.

**Setup:** Start at the selected mining spot or bank. Optional prospector outfit, mining gloves, Varrock armour, mining cape, or celestial/elven ring in inventory/equipped will be auto-equipped. No other items strictly required.

**Features:**
- Rock selector: Clay, Tin, Copper, Iron, Silver, Coal, Gold, Mithril, Adamantite, Runite, Amethyst
- Bank-location selector filtered to valid banks per rock (power-mine drop, Mining Guild, Crafting Guild, Varrock east/west)
- Power-mine (mine-and-drop) mode vs banking mode
- Auto-equips prospector/mining gear found in inventory
- Uses mining special attack at full spec
- Banks gems, geodes, clue scrolls and unidentified minerals; drops low-value ore
- Runite mode noted to not hop worlds
wasp-nightmare-zone-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Runs Nightmare Zone: talks to Dominic Onion, pays for and starts a customisable rumble dream, then fights inside the dream while managing boost potions, absorption/prayer potions, health, prayer flicking, special attacks, power-ups, and re-gearing. Outside the dream it withdraws potions from the barrels and unlocks/pays the coffer.

**Requirements:** Members; access to Nightmare Zone (quests unlocked for rumble bosses); Overload/Super ranging/Super magic potions and Absorption potions stored in the NMZ barrels (or Prayer potions for the first-time prayer mode); a Locator orb or Dwarven rock cake for lowering HP when not using prayer pots; coins in the coffer. Prayer levels checked (21/43) for Rapid Heal and Protect from Melee.

**Setup:** Start with your main weapon and shield equipped and the spec weapon in the inventory. Have boost and absorption potions in the barrels; if using prayer mode, carry prayer potions in inventory. Locator orb or Dwarven rock cake in inventory unless using prayer pots.

**Features:**
- Boost potion selector: Overload, Super ranging, Super magic
- Configurable boost amount (number of full potions to withdraw)
- Prayer-potion mode as an alternative to absorption potions (for first-timers without points)
- Optional power-ups toggle (active play vs AFK)
- Automatic absorption management and HP-lowering via locator orb / rock cake
- Prayer flicking and Protect-from-Melee handling based on Prayer level
- Special-attack usage and automatic re-gearing
- Handles coffer top-up/unlock and bank PIN
wasp-offerer-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Casts Sinister Offering or Demonic Offering to scatter bones/ashes for Prayer XP, withdrawing 3 remains plus the required runes from the bank each cycle, casting on cooldown, and handling level-ups and collect box. Spell is chosen automatically from the selected remains type.

**Requirements:** Members; Arceuus spellbook access with Sinister Offering (bones) or Demonic Offering (ashes) unlocked; Prayer/Magic levels for those spells; Wrath runes plus Blood runes (bones) or Soul runes (ashes); a supply of the chosen bones/ashes in the bank.

**Setup:** Bank at the selected bank chunk (default Grand Exchange) stocked with the chosen remains, Wrath runes, and Blood/Soul runes. No equipment specified.

**Features:**
- Bank-location selector (bank chunk)
- Remains selector: 5 ash types (Fiendish/Vile/Malicious/Abyssal/Infernal) and 9 bone types up to Superior dragon bones
- Auto-picks Demonic Offering for ashes, Sinister Offering for bones with matching alt rune
- Withdraws remains in sets of 3 with required runes
- Collect-box fallback when bank is empty
- Detects out-of-runes and screenshots the failure
- Profit tracking net of rune/remains cost
wasp-pickpocketer-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Pickpockets a chosen NPC (men/women, Master Farmers, Guards, Elves, or TzHaar), eating food and re-equipping dodgy necklaces as needed, opening coin pouches, banking or dropping loot, and optionally suiciding and looting its own gravestone on death. It also casts Shadow Veil and can use Redemption prayer in suicide mode.

**Requirements:** Members for Elves (Prifddinas), TzHaar (Mor Ul Rek), and Master Farmers loot; appropriate Thieving level for the target; Ardougne diary level affects coin-pouch capacity; Shadow Veil requires Arceuus spellbook and lava/earth-staff runes; Redemption prayer for suicide mode.

**Setup:** Start near the chosen NPC's city with food and dodgy necklaces in the bank/inventory. Optional rogue equipment (mask/top/trousers/gloves/boots), thieving cape, and a lava battlestaff/mystic lava staff/tome of fire for Shadow Veil are auto-detected and equipped.

**Features:**
- NPC selector: Men/women (Lumbridge), Master Farmers (Draynor), Guards (Falador), Elves (Prifddinas), TzHaar
- Suicide mode: pickpocket until death then loot own gravestone (auto-forced/disabled per NPC)
- Ardougne diary level selector for coin-pouch limit
- Configurable food amount, necklace amount, and minimum health
- Optional heal-to-full at bank
- Auto Shadow Veil casting when spellbook/staff available
- Auto-detects and equips rogue outfit for bonus loot
- Redemption prayer support in suicide mode
wasp-sawmill-runner-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Converts logs into planks by teleporting between the Grand Exchange bank and the sawmill: withdraws logs, teleports to the sawmill operator with a Ring of the elements, buys/makes planks, then Varrock-teleports back to bank the planks and repeat.

**Requirements:** Members; Magic level for Varrock teleport; a Ring of the elements charged for the sawmill (Earth) teleport; Law/Air/Fire runes (or a rune pouch) for Varrock teleport; coins to pay the sawmill operator; logs in the bank. Script is set up around Varrock region.

**Setup:** Bank at Grand Exchange stocked with logs and coins; carry a Ring of the elements (equipped or in inventory) and teleport runes / rune pouch. Uses Varrock teleport, so be on standard spellbook.

**Features:**
- Plank type selector: Normal, Oak, Teak, Mahogany
- Ring of the elements teleport to sawmill (inventory rub or equipped, with last-destination reuse)
- Varrock teleport back to Grand Exchange
- Buys/makes planks in bulk via the make interface
- Collect-box fallback when bank empty
- Profit tracking net of teleport and material costs
wasp-smelter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Smelts ores into bars (or soda ash + sand into molten glass) at a chosen furnace, withdrawing the correct ore ratios from the bank, opening the furnace make interface, waiting through the smelting, then depositing bars and repeating. Handles level-ups and can be chained with other scripts.

**Requirements:** Appropriate Smithing level for the bar (Crafting for molten glass); membership for some furnaces (Prifddinas); ores stocked in the bank in correct ratios (e.g. iron+coal for steel, ore+coal amounts for mithril/adamant/rune).

**Setup:** Bank near the chosen furnace stocked with the required ores (or soda ash + buckets of sand for glass). Start at/near that furnace's bank.

**Features:**
- Bar type selector: Bronze, Iron, Silver, Steel, Gold, Mithril, Adamantite, Runite, Molten glass
- Furnace selector: Al Kharid, Edgeville, Falador, Prifddinas
- Withdraws correct ore quantities and ratios per bar
- Bank-hover optimization while smelting
- Collect-box fallback when bank empty
- Withdraws finished (noted) bars on terminate
- Profit tracking per bar
wasp-stall-robber-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Steals repeatedly from a chosen market stall (tea, baker's/food, silk, or fruit), returning to a safe spot between thefts, and either banks the loot or drops it when the inventory fills. Uses per-stall cooldown timers and colour/uptext detection of the stall.

**Requirements:** Appropriate Thieving level for the stall; members for Ardougne/Hosidius stalls (fruit stall is in Hosidius); no special items needed.

**Setup:** Start at the selected stall's location (East Varrock tea stall, Ardougne market food/silk stalls, or Hosidius fruit stall). No inventory/equipment prep required.

**Features:**
- Stall selector: Tea (East Varrock), Food/baker's (Ardougne), Silk (Ardougne, safe-spots knights), Fruit (Hosidius)
- Optional bank-loot toggle (bank vs drop)
- Per-stall safe-spot walking between steals
- Per-stall steal cooldown timing
- Drops low-value items, keeps valuables when banking
- Handles level-up interruptions
wasp-tab-maker-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Automates mass-producing teleport/spell tablets at a Player-Owned House lectern by repeatedly making tablets from soft clay and restocking clay either from Phials (via noted soft clay) or by having the demon butler (servant) fetch clay from the bank. It tracks tabs made and estimated profit as the difference between tablet and soft-clay value.

**Requirements:** Membership, a Player-Owned House with a lectern and exit portal configured in the house builder, ability to cast the chosen tablet spell, coins for the spell cost, and (butler method) a demon butler/servant with money in its money bag.

**Setup:** Configure your house layout (lectern and exit portal) in the house builder tab. Phials method: bring noted soft clay, coins, and the required spell available. Butler method: keep soft clay in the bank, pre-set the butler's last fetch task, keep coins in the servant's money bag (replenished manually), and fetch quantities that leave no remainder.

**Features:**
- Two clay-restock methods: Phials (noted clay) or Demon butler
- Lectern type selector: Standard, Ancient, Lunar, Arceuus
- Spell tablet selector populated from the chosen lectern
- Saves NPC/lectern/tablet choices to config between runs
- Automatic level-up handling
- Profit and action tracking based on live item prices
wasp-wintertodt-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Fights the Wintertodt firemaking minigame end to end: banks for supplies, enters the boss room, chops bruma roots, optionally fletches them into kindling, lights and feeds the brazier, fixes it when broken, and heals with food or self-brewed rejuvenation potions while managing warmth. It switches braziers/sides and safely leaves the room on breaks and script end.

**Requirements:** Firemaking level to enter Wintertodt (50), members' access to Great Kourend/Zeah, and completion of Druidic Ritual to brew rejuvenation potions (otherwise it uses Brewma to concoct them).

**Setup:** Have a knife, tinderbox, and hammer in the inventory and an axe equipped; keep a tinderbox and hammer (or a Bruma torch equipped in place of the tinderbox) available in the bank. It logs in on Wintertodt-friendly worlds (307, 309, 311).

**Features:**
- Full brazier cycle: cut, fletch, light, fix, feed
- Warmth management with in-house rejuvenation potion brewing (herbs + vials)
- Self-heals and abandons unsafe actions when hit
- Automatic side/brazier switching and safespot walking
- Special-attack weapon usage when spec is full
- Break/sleep handling that retreats to a safespot or leaves the room
- Deposits the supply crate and withdraws tinderbox/hammer automatically
wasp-woodcutter-by-torwent:
**What it does:** Chops a configurable tree type at a chosen location, then either power-drops logs, banks them, fletches them, or takes them to a sawmill to make planks (including plank-sack support). It picks up bird nests, uses woodcutting special-attack axes, and tracks logs/planks cut and profit.

**Requirements:** Woodcutting level for the chosen tree (e.g. oak 15, willow 30, teak/maple, mahogany, yew 60, magic 75), members' access for member areas/trees (Prifddinas, Woodcutting Guild, Isle of Souls, etc.), and coins for sawmill plank costs when that mode is used.

**Setup:** Start near the chosen tree/bank location. Optionally keep a full lumberjack outfit and strung rabbit foot in the inventory to auto-equip, a knife for fletching, coins for the sawmill, and a plank sack if using one.

**Features:**
- 8 tree types (regular through magic) with per-tree locations
- Multiple locations including Woodcutting Guild, Prifddinas, Varrock, Draynor, Seers, Castle Wars, Ruins of Uzer
- Powercut (drop) mode vs. banking mode
- Sawmill plank-making with plank-sack emptying
- Optional log fletching
- Bird nest pickup toggle
- Auto-equips lumberjack gear; uses special-attack axe
- Saves tree/location/toggle choices to config
waspquests-by-bootje:
**What it does:** Launches the WaspQuests QuestSolver — a sequential quest-solving engine with solvers for **162 quests and miniquests**, from Tutorial Island and all the F2P classics up to Desert Treasure, Dragon Slayer II and Song of the Elves. Pick a quest (and optionally a starting step) in the GUI and it walks, talks, fights and quests its way through.

**Requirements:** Per-quest skill/item requirements are shown in the info box in the GUI. Some solvers buy missing items via the Grand Exchange backend.

**Setup:** Nothing to install — the library (v0.9.12) is vendored in `Includes/WaspQuests`, no downloads at runtime. This script sanity-checks the install and opens the QuestSolver in a new Simba tab.

**Features:**
- 162 quest/miniquest solvers, including ironman variants that gather their own items
- Step selector: resume a quest from any step
- Sequential state machine: every step has a clear success condition and is retried on failure
- Own overworld webwalker (bundled collision map + web graph) for cross-map travel
- Grand Exchange integration for buying required items
xerics-talisman-by-bootje:
**What it does:** Thieves the Stone chests in the Lizardman Temple (Chasm of Fire area) to obtain Xeric's talisman (inert), repeatedly picklocking the chest, auto-dismantling any talisman found, and dropping unwanted loot. It walks back and re-enters the lizard dwelling if the player exits the underground area.

**Requirements:** Thieving level and members' access for Stone chests, a lockpick, and food (chests can poison and damage the player).

**Setup:** Start with a lockpick in the inventory, bring food (auto-eaten below 70% HP) and antipoison handling, and begin near the Stone chest in the Lizardman Temple.

**Features:**
- Option to stop automatically upon obtaining a talisman
- Auto-dismantles inert talismans when kept going
- Auto-eats food below 70% HP and cures poison
- Drops a configured list of junk seeds/gems/bolt tips when inventory is full
- Auto-walks back and re-enters the dungeon if the player leaves
- Adjusts camera zoom automatically for consistent clicking
hcim-helper-by-elixer:
**What it does:** A helper for hardcore ironman play. It runs alongside your manual session and stays completely idle until you use one of its features. First feature: an **emergency teleport keybind** — press the configured key and it instantly clicks the configured inventory slot, where you keep your emergency teleport (teleport tab, ectophial, royal seed pod: anything with a left-click teleport). Typical key-to-click latency is a few milliseconds, far inside one game tick.

**Requirements:** An emergency teleport item in the chosen inventory slot. Works with the inventory tab closed (it opens it first, which costs a fraction of a second — keep the inventory tab open when it matters).

**Setup:** Pick the keybind (~ or F1-F12) and the inventory slot in the GUI (default: bottom-left), put your teleport there, press Start and keep playing by hand.

**Features:**
- Emergency teleport keybind: one key press = one click, nothing else — no antiban, no idle behaviour
- Keybind and inventory slot selectable in the GUI
- Opens the inventory tab first if another tab is active
- 1.2s cooldown against double-fires; logs the measured reaction time per press
- Warns at startup if the teleport slot is empty
